questions.question: Explain the difference between shallow copy and deep copy with an example.
keyPoints: Define shallow copy - copies only member values including pointer addresses
Define deep copy - creates independent copy including heap data
Example showing pointer sharing in shallow copy causing issues
Example showing independent memory in deep copy

questions.question: What is the Rule of Three in C++? Why is it important?
keyPoints: State Rule of Three: destructor, copy constructor, copy assignment
Explain why all three are needed for resource management
Example of class needing custom implementations
Consequence of not following Rule of Three

questions.question: Explain when copy constructor is called vs when assignment operator is invoked.
keyPoints: Copy constructor: new object creation from existing object
Assignment operator: assigning to already existing object
Key difference: object existence before operation

questions.question: What is self-assignment and why should assignment operator handle it?
keyPoints: Show how to check for self-assignment
Example of problematic scenario with dynamic memory

questions.question: Write the signature of copy assignment operator and explain each part.
keyPoints: Parameter: const ClassName& to avoid modification

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'this' pointer in object assignment context?
keyPoints: Used to distinguish member variables from parameters
Used for returning reference from assignment operator

questions.question: Explain member-wise assignment performed by default assignment operator.
Write a C++ class with dynamically allocated memory and implement proper copy constructor and assignment operator to perform deep copy. Also explain the importance of these implementations.
keyPoints: Class with dynamic memory (e.g., String class or Array class)
Proper copy constructor with deep copy
Proper assignment operator with deep copy

questions.question: Explain the Rule of Five in C++11. How does it differ from Rule of Three? Write a program demonstrating all five special member functions.
keyPoints: Explain Rule of Three vs Rule of Five
When to use default/delete special functions

questions.question: Differentiate between copy constructor and assignment operator with suitable code examples showing when each is invoked.
What is copy elision? Explain with examples. How does it optimize performance in object assignment and initialization?
keyPoints: Common cases: return value optimization, copy elision in initialization
